title: Unexpected catalyst stability
seed_task: A chemistry lab is testing a cheap nickel catalyst for a reaction that normally needs palladium. The nickel catalyst performs modestly at first, but after several cycles it becomes more stable and slightly more selective. The team suspects contamination, surface restructuring, or a measurement artifact. Analyze the situation and design next steps.
final_artifact: research memo with the most plausible mechanisms, discriminating experiments, and the next experiment to run

title: Sleep study paradox
seed_task: A small sleep study finds that one night of mild sleep restriction hurts recall on a standard word-list task, but improves performance on a creative association task. The effect is noisy but appears in two pilot cohorts. Analyze what might be happening and propose a follow-up study.
final_artifact: study plan that separates mechanism hypotheses from measurement artifacts

title: Antibiotic pulse resilience
seed_task: A microbial community in a bioreactor recovers faster after repeated short antibiotic pulses than after a single long exposure with the same total dose. The lab wants to know whether this reflects selection, community buffering, stress priming, or sampling bias.
final_artifact: experimental plan with controls and expected signatures for competing explanations

title: Battery electrolyte anomaly
seed_task: A battery team observes unexpectedly high ion transport in an electrolyte blend that should be too viscous. The result is useful if real, but the measurement setup is sensitive to temperature gradients and electrode wetting.
final_artifact: technical memo proposing mechanisms, artifacts to rule out, and a validation sequence

title: Tumor organoid stiffness response
seed_task: Tumor organoids in a softer matrix become less invasive in one cell line but more invasive in another. Both lines share the same driver mutation but differ in immune signaling markers. The team needs a hypothesis for why stiffness effects split by line.
final_artifact: research hypothesis memo with testable mechanisms and minimum experiments

title: Cheap alloy toughness
seed_task: A materials group sees higher fracture toughness in a low-cost alloy batch after a supplier change. Chemical assays show a trace impurity that is usually treated as undesirable. The effect could be real, batch noise, or processing variation.
final_artifact: mechanism and validation plan for whether the impurity is causally useful

title: Pollinator behavior shift
seed_task: Drone observations suggest pollinators visit urban rooftop gardens in shorter bursts than ground-level gardens, but total daily visits are similar. The team wants to know whether this changes pollination success or only changes movement patterns.
final_artifact: field study design with hypotheses, confounds, and measurement priorities

title: Model organism lifespan signal
seed_task: A lab testing a metabolic intervention in worms sees lifespan extension only when plates are handled by one technician. The technician follows the same protocol but tends to stage transfers earlier in the day.
final_artifact: failure-analysis memo that preserves useful hypotheses while controlling for handling artifacts

title: Local-first sync conflicts
seed_task: Design the sync strategy for a local-first notes app where users can edit offline on phone and laptop, then reconnect days later. Notes may contain text, tags, and embedded checklist items. The team wants simple behavior that users can understand.
final_artifact: technical design recommendation with conflict strategy, risks, and implementation outline

title: Marketplace rate limiter
seed_task: A marketplace API is being abused by a few sellers who scrape competitor prices every minute. Legitimate sellers also run frequent inventory sync jobs. Design a rate-limiting approach that reduces abuse without breaking normal automation.
final_artifact: rate-limiter design with signals, policy choices, rollout risks, and monitoring

title: Silent model-eval drift
seed_task: An internal ML eval dashboard has started showing stable aggregate scores while users report worse answers. The eval set, judge model, and model routing may all have changed recently. Propose a debugging plan.
final_artifact: debugging plan that prioritizes likely causes and separates metric drift from product drift

title: Background job migration
seed_task: A team is moving background jobs from a monolith cron runner to a queue-based worker system. Some jobs are idempotent, some send emails, and some update billing state. The migration must avoid duplicate side effects.
final_artifact: migration design with sequencing, safeguards, and failure-mode handling

title: Plugin sandbox risk
seed_task: A product wants third-party plugins that can read user documents and call approved APIs. The business wants a broad plugin ecosystem; security wants tight isolation and review. Design a first version of the plugin sandbox.
final_artifact: v1 sandbox architecture with permission model, review flow, and abuse risks

title: Telemetry backpressure
seed_task: A streaming telemetry service receives bursts from edge devices. During incidents, the bursts are most important, but they also overload storage and delay dashboards. Design a backpressure and degradation strategy.
final_artifact: systems design memo with data-priority policy, degradation behavior, and observability

title: Tutoring chatbot learning claim
seed_task: A draft paper claims a tutoring chatbot improves learning because students who use it more have higher exam scores. Usage is voluntary, and stronger students may use the tool differently. Critique the argument and propose a better study.
final_artifact: review memo identifying causal weaknesses and a stronger evaluation design

title: Metric improves productivity claim
seed_task: A management proposal argues that adding daily individual productivity metrics will improve team output because measured work improves. It cites examples from sales teams and call centers. Critique the claim for a software engineering organization.
final_artifact: argument critique with hidden assumptions, likely failure modes, and a better measurement approach

title: Climate intervention simulation claim
seed_task: A proposal argues that a regional climate intervention should proceed to field trials because simulations show large expected benefits. The simulations rely on uncertain cloud feedback assumptions and sparse regional data.
final_artifact: critique memo distinguishing what the simulations support from what remains unknown

title: Universal compute grant claim
seed_task: A policy essay claims that giving every researcher a fixed cloud-compute grant would democratize AI science. It gives compelling anecdotes but little analysis of bottlenecks, misuse, or allocation design.
final_artifact: critique and strengthened version of the policy argument

title: Biotech platform or product
seed_task: A small biotech team has one promising platform technology and one narrow therapeutic lead. Investors want a clear product story; the scientists believe the platform could open several programs. Decide how the team should allocate the next six months.
final_artifact: strategy memo with recommended allocation, milestones, and kill criteria

title: Open-source roadmap pressure
seed_task: An open-source infrastructure project is getting enterprise adoption. Maintainers are pressured to add admin features, but the community wants reliability and better documentation. The team has limited maintainer time.
final_artifact: roadmap recommendation balancing enterprise pull, community health, and maintainer capacity

title: City heat resilience choice
seed_task: A city has funding for heat resilience but cannot do everything. Options include tree planting, cooling centers, reflective roofs, tenant protections, and emergency alerting. The mayor wants visible action before next summer.
final_artifact: prioritized action plan with tradeoffs, sequencing, and equity risks

title: Lab time allocation
seed_task: A research lab has six months before renewal review. It can either deepen one promising line, explore two risky adjacent ideas, or write up existing results. The PI wants discovery upside but also needs credible progress.
final_artifact: lab strategy recommendation with portfolio allocation and review-ready milestones

title: Negative control signal
seed_task: In a biology experiment, the negative control repeatedly shows a weak but nonzero signal. The team has checked the obvious contamination sources and found nothing. Some members want to discard the run; others think the control may reveal something real.
final_artifact: interpretation memo with artifact checks, real-signal hypotheses, and decision criteria

title: Engagement improves after feature removal
seed_task: A consumer app removes a popular feature because of maintenance cost. Support complaints spike, but two weeks later retention and session quality improve. The product team does not know whether removal helped, users adapted, or the metrics are misleading.
final_artifact: product analysis memo with competing explanations and follow-up measurement plan
